model: ΔSOH = β · k^0.5  (γ=0.5 fixed, D_k=k unit-cycle damage)

dataset.name: Severson et al. (2019) Nature Energy 4:383–391
dataset.manufacturer: A123, single test facility (MIT) — NOT cross-manufacturer
dataset.honest_note: β variance is partly protocol-driven (varied charge C-rates). LFP feature range wide due to flat 3.3 V plateau. Cross-manufacturer generalisation NOT tested.

check_A_feature_utility.verdict: Feature-mapped β₀ WORSE than plain mean at n=20 (ΔR²=-8.481). Feature→β regression is WEAK on held-out cells (R²_held_out=-4.822). The ΔQ(V) feature does not reliably predict β for LFP cells. LFP plateau noise (flat 3.3 V) likely inflates variance non-informatively. R²_feat→β: train_avg=0.095 / held_out=-4.822  (7/124 cells extrapolate outside training F-range).  β_feature MAE=90.8% vs β_mean MAE=55.6%.
check_A_feature_utility.note: Method B uses prior-mean for n<20 cycles. Training and held-out R² reported separately to detect overfitting.

check_B_deadband_utility.verdict: Dead-band makes NO MEASURABLE DIFFERENCE: n=10 Δ=+0.000, n=20 Δ=-0.000. Nearly all innovations exceed τ — no updates gated. Dead-band τ=0.005 SOH: 3425/97067 cycle-innovations below τ (3.5% gated). Median max|innovation| per cell = 0.1051 SOH.

check_C_fewshot_framing.verdict: Structural — always fails. NOT zero-cycle prediction. R²(n=0) reflects population prior only.

check_gamma_adequacy.verdict: γ=0.5 gives adequate fits (R²≥0.70) for 2/124 cells (median R²=0.357). Poor fits for many cells suggest the power-law model may be misspecified for some LFP degradation trajectories.
